calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment -Unlimited Civil Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Sasimu LLC, a suspended limited liability company, dba World of Charms.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

Denis Klavdianos, Esq. appeared with plaintiff A&D Gem Corp, a corporation.
No appearance by defendant Sasimu LLC, a suspended limited liability company, dba World of Charms.
Plaintiff's complaint for Money Due for Goods Sold and Delivered was filed on August 10, 2016.
Substitute service on defendant Sasimu LLC, a suspended limited liability company, dba World of Charms, took place on August 25, 2016 and was effectuated on September 5, 2016.
Default as to defendant Sasimu LLC, a suspended limited liability company, dba World of Charms, was entered on December 15, 2016.
DOE defendants were dismissed on April 25, 2019.
Dismissal without prejudice as to defendants (1) World of Charms, Inc., a suspended corporation, f/k/a WOC, Inc., dba World of Charms, (2) Namer Inc., (3) Sarit Simhayoff-Cohen and (4) Zack Namer was filed on August 30, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff A&D Gem Corp., a corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant Sasimu LLC, a suspended limited liability company, dba World of Charms, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $137,172.86.
Prejudgment Interest - $61,029.92.
Attorney Fees - $1,000.00.
Costs - $655.90.

Total - $199,858.68.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Zack Namer and Sarit Simhayoff-Cohen's demurrer to the both causes of action in the complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. While it would have been clearer if plaintiff A & D Gem Corp. alleged its alter ego allegations by referring to the demurring defendants by, the complaint adequately alleges alter ego liability as to the demurring defendants. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963. (302/HK)
